_comment: Static conflict rules. In production this would be backed by a live drug database (e.g. RxNorm). Drug names must match canonical form (lowercase).

blacklisted_combinations.reason: Concurrent use significantly increases bleeding risk without anticoagulation monitoring.
ACE inhibitor + potassium-sparing diuretic combination raises hyperkalaemia risk in CKD patients.
Metformin must be held before iodinated contrast administration (lactic acidosis risk).
Sevelamer chelates fluoroquinolones, significantly reducing absorption.
Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4, raising simvastatin levels and myopathy risk.
Loop diuretic + aminoglycoside combination increases ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity risk.
